regions.text: A record was made by making cuts on a stick. The number of cuts equaled the number of things recorded.
The stick was cut in half so each half had half of every cut. These two half sticks were tallies.
If put side by side, the halves of the cuts came together. They tallied. Each man took one tally for the record.
tally: one sort of record. FUTURE: will equal, will tally. PRESENT: equal(s), tally(ies). PAST: equaled, tallied.
